frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: positive temporal integration biasing current percept toward prior stimulus

frameworks.name: adaptation aftereffects
frameworks.one_line: negative repulsion of current percept away from recent stimulus history

frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: coding strategy calibrated to temporal statistics of attribute stability

core_question: Does the visual system deploy different temporal coding strategies (integration vs differentiation) for stable versus changeable facial attributes?
key_assumptions: perceptual systems trade off signal integration against change sensitivity
facial identity is temporally stable whereas expression and gaze are changeable
priming/serial dependence and adaptation reflect distinct coding strategies
manipulations: facial attribute type judged (identity vs expression vs gaze)
prior-stimulus history preceding the judged face
key_findings: identity judgments showed positive (attractive) serial dependence toward prior identity
expression judgments showed negative adaptation aftereffects (repulsion from prior expression)
the dissociation held across five experiments
interpretations: the visual system uses different coding strategies calibrated to each attribute's temporal statistics
stable attributes recruit integration while changeable attributes recruit differentiation
